channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Hiding kids' 'gender identity' from parents is common in blue state fighting Trump on trans issues: watchdog
item.description: A watchdog group that tracks gender ideology in schools found more than 50 school districts in Maine have policies that exclude parents from learning their children have gender dysphoria.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newsom declares state of emergency to 'fast-track' wildfire measures after Trump attacks over previous blazes
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom declared a state of emergency on Saturday in a bid to remove red tape blocking funding for wildfire relief efforts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's next first speech to Congress is bound to have little resemblance to his last first one
item.description: When President Donald Trump speaks to a joint session of Congress on Tuesday, his words are bound to be far different from when he addressed lawmakers at the beginning of his first term.
item.dc:creator: Calvin Woodward, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How to watch and what to know about Trump's Tuesday address to a Joint Session of Congress
item.description: On Tuesday, President Donald Trump will address a Joint Session of Congress; here is how to watch on Fox News Channel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Debate over whether to ban handgun sales to teens could soon head to the Supreme Court
item.description: Competing rulings from federal court jurisdictions could tee up the Supreme Court to make its own ruling on whether young people under 21 can legally purchase handguns, something that has been illegal at the federal level for many decades.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House panel on gov't secrets unlikely to tackle UFOs: Journalist
item.description: The House's Task Force on the Declassification of Federal Secrets is also tasked with looking for files on the deaths of JFK and MLK.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump takes actions to increase lumber supplies and curb wood imports
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump on Saturday signed a pair of actions to increase domestic lumber production, including a directive for the Commerce Department to investigate the possible harms that lumber imports pose to national security. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE structure, authority emerge as biggest DC mystery under Trump
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency’s (DOGE) structure and authority has become a central mystery in the barrage of lawsuits seeking to stymie its operations.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Threatening letter sent to northern Colorado family over MAGA banner
item.description: A Severance family says they recently received a threatening letter in the mail, in response to a "Make America Great Again" banner posted in their front yard.
item.dc:creator: Greg Nieto, NewsNation

item.title: Pennsylvania Republicans who narrowly won their House seats feel the heat of early votes back home
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Newly minted U.S. Rep. Rob Bresnahan swore he wouldn't support gutting government benefits such as Medicaid that residents of his northeastern Pennsylvania district rely on. Then the first-term Republican voted for a bill that could do just that.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks Trump order threatening funding for institutions that offer care for transgender youth
item.description: A judge has continued to block President Donald Trump’s plan to pull federal funding from institutions that provide gender-affirming care for transgender youth. U.S. District Court Judge Lauren King in Seattle issued the permanent injunction late Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Firings at US weather and oceans agency risk lives and economy, former agency heads warn
item.description: Elon Musk has repeatedly defended federal workforce cuts by his Department of Government Efficiency as “common sense.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: USAID cuts are already hitting countries around the world. Here are 20 projects that have closed
item.description: Countries around the world already are feeling the impact of the Trump administration’s decision to eliminate more than 90 percent of foreign aid contracts and cut some $60 billion in funding.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump's Oval Office thrashing of Zelenskyy shows limits of Western allies' ability to sway US leader
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — All it took was 90 seconds for weeks of tortured diplomacy to unwind in spectacular fashion.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Zelenskyy moves on after Oval Office blowout with Trump
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has thanked the American people and leadership and voiced hope for “strong relations,” a day after an astonishing Oval Office blowout with U.S. President Donald Trump that left many uncertain where the once staunch allies stood.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: State Sen. Shannon Grove introduces bill for additional protection for sex trafficking victims, fines for perpetrators
item.description: Grove's previous proposals -- which were signed into law -- exclude cases with 16 and 17-year-old victims.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani in second place in new poll
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – It is under four months away until voters hit the polls for the Democratic primary for New York City mayor. New polling from the Honan Strategy Group showed some interesting results about the race.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump and Zelenskyy through the years: From a 'perfect' call to an Oval Office meltdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The first time he spoke to Volodymyr Zelenskyy, President Donald Trump tried to pressure Ukraine's new leader to dig up dirt on Joe Biden ahead of the 2020 election. It was a 2019 phone call that eventually sparked an impeachment.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy leaves White House without signing minerals deal after Oval Office blowup
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump berated Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for being “disrespectful” Friday in an extraordinary Oval Office meeting, then abruptly called off the signing of a minerals deal that Trump said would have moved Ukraine closer to ending its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, ZEKE MILLER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: European leaders pledge to stand by Ukraine after confrontational Oval Office meeting with Trump
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — European leaders pledged late Friday to stand by Ukraine in the wake of the contentious Oval Office meeting between U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO and SUSIE BLANN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What they said: Trump, Zelenskyy and Vance's heated argument in the Oval Office
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance on Friday berated Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy over the war in Ukraine, accusing him of not showing gratitude after he challenged Vance on the question of diplomacy with Russia's Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump and Zelenskyy's meeting turns into a heated argument
item.description: A meeting between President Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy grew contentious, with Trump telling Zelenskyy that "you're not acting at all thankful" for U.S. support.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: Hundreds of weather forecasters fired in latest wave of DOGE cuts
item.description: Hundreds of weather forecasters and other federal National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration employees on probationary status have been fired, lawmakers and weather experts say.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Prince Harry's immigration status under fire over drugs, possible lies
item.description: Prince Harry's immigration status has come under fire after he admitted to recreational use of marijuana, cocaine and psychedelics.
item.dc:creator: Tom Palmer, NewsNation

item.title: Rosen, Republicans introduce plan to ban DeepSeek on government devices
item.description: Democratic Nevada Sen. Jacky Rosen joined two Republicans on Thursday to introduce a bill in Congress to ban the Chinese artificial intelligence platform DeepSeek from government devices and networks.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How Trump’s crackdown plays into misperceptions about immigrants and crime
item.description: The White House portrays its immigration crackdown as a success. Critics say the administration is trying to look tough for the cameras, and worry about the “tail wagging the dog.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose, MPR News

item.title: 2026 gubernatorial race heats up as DeSantis shifts focus to Casey, Trump backs Donalds
item.description: It may appear to be distant, but the 2026 gubernatorial race is already heating up. President Donald Trump has put his full support behind Congressman Byron Donalds, but Gov. Ron DeSantis does not seem to be on board with that choice.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Zelensky expected to visit White House amid push for minerals deal
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is expected to visit the White House on Friday as Kyiv and the Trump administration work to finalize an agreement on critical minerals.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump defends Musk’s ‘genius’ email to federal workers
item.description: President Trump on Monday defended tech billionaire Elon Musk after pushback over his email demanding federal workers list five accomplishments from the past week and in doing so, raising the of risk losing their jobs. “There was a lot of genius in sending it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC
